programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier
programme.desc: Jane and Charlotte protect their herd from bears, three generations of the Kilcher family build a shack on Perl Island, and Otto has a surprise for Charlotte

programme.title: Edge of Alaska
programme.desc: Jeremy gets his farmstead up and running, but unfinished business gets in the way. Plus, bears pose a threat to Jenny and her newborn baby while stranded on an island in a storm

programme.title: Expedition Bigfoot
programme.desc: Mireya and Ronny return to where they think they heard whistles and saw glowing eyes. Meanwhile, Mireya agrees to act as bait in order to lure out Bigfoot

programme.title: Loch Ness Monster: New Evidence
programme.desc: Top scientists reveal how a revolutionary study and advanced DNA technology solved the mystery of the legendary Loch Ness monster

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Jackson Galaxy helps families calm their unruly felines, meeting Debbie who is torn between her boyfriend and her incontinent cat Blinkie. Plus, Krisi and Jason want to get married, but standing in their way is her energetic feline Frankie

programme.title: Crikey! Its the Irwins
programme.desc: Steve Irwin loved sea turtles. Terri and Robert continue his legacy as they transport three rescued sea turtles to Lady Elliot Island on the Great Barrier Reef - for the first time, by air. This complex mission is vital in order to help conserve these beautiful but endangered animals. While Robert and Terri take to the skies, Bindi and the team are nervously waiting for the birth of Australia Zoos first Sumatran tiger cub in three years. Richie from the rescue unit helps with a baby wallabys struggle for survival

programme.title: Lone Star Law
programme.desc: During wildfire season, warden Dustin Delgado catches people throwing lit cigarettes. Warden Cynde Aguilar helps hunt down the criminal of a high-speed chase

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Dr Jeff must act fast when a dog is rushed into the clinic foaming at the mouth - and when he meets Penelope the Pig, he faces a surgery he has never performed before

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: A dog is rushed into the clinic for treatment after eating a box of chocolates infused with marijuana, while a puppy is rescued from being put down at a shelter in Oklahoma

programme.title: Pit Bulls and Parolees
programme.desc: Lizzy tries to track down the owner of a dog tied up outside the rescue centre. An older couple come looking for their first dog

programme.title: Pit Bulls and Parolees
programme.desc: Two strays lead Lizzy and the Villalobos staff on a wild goose chase. A grandmother comes in looking for another canine companion, but her dog Daisy has other plans

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy helps Buddhist couple Tom and Pola, whose tranquil lives have been ruined by their adopted cat

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy helps Jeff, a photographer whose business is at risk because of his blood-thirsty cat, Buster

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete Nelson builds a barn-like birdhouse for two retirees on their Wisconsin farm, designing three luxury pods and a deck overlooking their stable of alpacas

programme.title: Wild Appalachia
programme.desc: Documentary about the rarely-seen wildlife of Americas most popular forests, revealing events in the habitat of North Americas Appalachian Mountains

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: A game of underwater copycat shows how smart dolphins really are. Plus, how hyenas can eat pretty much anything and geckos ability to apparently defy gravity

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.sub-title: Space Bugs and Squirrel Mind Maps
programme.desc: A squirrels ability to hide up to 10,000 nuts in one season - and still find them again. Plus, alligators fondness for music and whether a bug can survive in space

programme.title: Little Giants
programme.desc: The team is in Madagascar to study the recently discovered Goodmans mouse lemur. One of the worlds smallest primates, it is incredible agile and ferocious

programme.title: Little Giants
programme.desc: Bradley and Billy are hunting for dragons deep in the rainforests of Thailand. The flying dragon rules the jungle canopy, gliding and dive-bombing its prey

programme.title: Alaska: Surviving the Last Frontier
programme.desc: Following four groups of ordinary people as they travel into the wilderness of Alaska, where they must find a new home in the inhospitable environment and deal with the threat posed by bears, glaciers and their own fears

programme.desc: Brett Raymer and Wayde King are tasked with transporting a fully operational beer fermenter before heading south to create an iconic doughnut-shaped tank

programme.desc: A couple ignore warnings to leave their home but call in the paranormal team when one of them awakes with a cross painted on their forehead

programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier
programme.desc: The Kilchers expand a road with the help of a detonation expert. Meanwhile, Atz Sr and Bonnie embark work together to salvage vital resources

programme.title: Crikey! Its the Irwins
programme.desc: Terri returns to her hometown of Oregon to help Brady, a cougar in need of a health check. At the zoo, Bindi bathes an echidna with bad skin allergies

programme.title: Sorry!
programme.desc: Timothy decides to leave home, but his mother is outraged when she learns of his decision to share a flat with two young women

programme.title: Elaine Paige: 60 Years on Stage
programme.desc: The musical theatre star chats to Zoe Ball about her career, sharing anecdotes and insights from major West End and Broadway productions she has appeared in, including Hair, Evita, Cats, Chess, Piaf, Anything Goes, Sunset Boulevard and Follies. As well as stories about meeting royalty, working with Queen and Stephen Sondheim, and the instant fame that winning the much-coveted role of Eva Peron brought her, there are clips of some of the sensational show tunes she made famous

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: An arsonist terrorises a housing estate, and Hetty goes undercover in a quilt-making circle in her search for the culprit, while Robert is confined at home with a broken collarbone and has to content himself with Polaroid shots of his burgeoning runner bean crop

programme.title: Angela Rippon: Portrait
programme.desc: Paul Wyeth paints a portrait of the newsreader and broadcaster. First broadcast in1979

programme.title: Imagine
programme.sub-title: Rose Wylie: This Rose Is Blooming
programme.desc: Alan Yentob meets the painter, known for her exuberant, large-scale canvases that have been exhibited and sold all over the world. He discovers how her memories and experiences - including her childhood in the Blitz during the Second World War, have helped mould her into the artist she is today, and how she transforms the stuff of everyday experience into new and hitherto unseen painterly visions

programme.title: Elaine Paige: 60 Years on Stage
programme.desc: The musical theatre star chats to Zoe Ball about her career, sharing anecdotes and insights from major West End and Broadway productions she has appeared in, including Hair, Evita, Cats, Chess, Piaf, Anything Goes, Sunset Boulevard and Follies. As well as stories about meeting royalty, working with Queen and Stephen Sondheim, and the instant fame that winning the much-coveted role of Eva Peron brought her, there are clips of some of the sensational show tunes she made famous

programme.title: Elaine Paige Remembers - A Night on the Town
programme.desc: Elaine Paige looks back on the 1983 musical spectacular, filmed with a cast of singers and dancers such as Ann Reinking, Hinton Battle, Frank Gorshin and Eartha Kitt

programme.title: A Night on the Town
programme.desc: In the 1980s, a photographer and an advertising designer trying on clothes in a vintage clothing store find themselves transported to the 1930s. Musical showcasing the work of Irving Berlin, Noel Coward, George Gershwin and Cole Porter, starring Hinton Battle, Lewis Collins, Frank Gorshin, Eartha Kitt, John Moffatt, Elaine Paige, Ann Reinking and Bobby Short. First aired in 1983

programme.title: Charles Dance Remembers - Little Eyolf
programme.desc: The actor reflects on Michael Meyers 1982 adaptation of Henrik Ibsens play, in which he played Borghejm in one of his first major TV roles, Charles reveals why actors are constantly attracted to Ibsens writing, how the work was filmed in a way to keep it deliberately theatrical, and why the play and the subjects it tackles are as relevant as ever

programme.title: Little Eyolf
programme.desc: Michael Meyers 1982 adaptation of Henrik Ibsens psychological study of grief and guilt, starring Diana Rigg, Anthony Hopkins, Peggy Ashcroft and Charles Dance

programme.title: Mindful Escapes: Breathe, Release, Restore
programme.desc: Mindfulness expert Andy Puddicombe explains how watching scenes from nature can help bring feelings of positivity, happiness and wellbeing to the viewer

programme.title: Mindful Escapes: Breathe, Release, Restore
programme.desc: Mindfulness expert Andy Puddicombe explains how watching scenes from nature can help bring feelings of positivity, happiness and wellbeing to the viewer

programme.title: A Night on the Town
programme.desc: In the 1980s, a photographer and an advertising designer trying on clothes in a vintage clothing store find themselves transported to the 1930s. Musical showcasing the work of Irving Berlin, Noel Coward, George Gershwin and Cole Porter, starring Hinton Battle, Lewis Collins, Frank Gorshin, Eartha Kitt, John Moffatt, Elaine Paige, Ann Reinking and Bobby Short. First aired in 1983

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the wildlife of Australia, a land cast adrift at the time of the dinosaurs and isolated for millions of years, leaving weird and wonderful animals found nowhere else on Earth. In its jungles, a cassowary - one of the most dangerous birds in the world - stands six feet tall, while kangaroos and wombats brave snowstorms, and gum-tree forests are filled with predators. In its red desert heart, reptiles drink through their skin and huge flocks of wild budgerigars swirl in search of water. On secret islands Tasmanian devils roam, and offshore thousands of sharks gather for a rare event

programme.title: Treasures of Ancient Egypt
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke looks at art from one of Ancient Egypts most opulent eras, beginning with the troubling psychological portraits of tyrant Senwosret III, who ruled during the Middle Kingdom, through to the golden mask of Tutankhamun, which was created hundreds of years later. He also explores architectural wonders, exquisite tombs and a lost city, where an artistic revolution took place and a new creative style was developed under Akhenaten and Nefertiti

programme.title: Call My Bluff
programme.desc: Robert Robinson presides over another duel of words and wits as team captains Frank Muir and Patrick Campbell are joined by guests Lesley-Anne Down, Anthony Valentine, Jilly Cooper and Quentin Bell. First broadcast in 1976

programme.title: Face the Music
programme.desc: Joseph Cooper invites viewers to match their musical wits against Joyce Grenfell, Paul Jennings and Robin Ray. With guest musician Gerald Moore. First broadcast in 1981

programme.title: Pompeii: The Mystery of the People Frozen in Time
programme.desc: Documentary in which Margaret Mountford visits the Roman town of Pompeii, where in 79AD the catastrophic eruption of Vesuvius encased its citizens in lava, creating body casts that populate the ruins. No one has yet been able to fully explain how these people became frozen in time, so while forensic scientists peer beneath the plaster to rebuild the faces of two victims and recreate the final moments of the townsfolk, Margaret dispels the myths surrounding the events in 79AD and explores the lives of the individuals who lived in the enigmatic city

programme.title: What Do Artists Do All Day?
programme.desc: Internationally renowned abstract artist Frank Bowling became the first black Royal Academician in 2005. As a major retrospective opens at Tate Britain, Brenda Emmanus meets him to look back over his long career and there are interviews with critics and fellow artists to discuss the significance of his work in the history of British art

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the wildlife of Australia, a land cast adrift at the time of the dinosaurs and isolated for millions of years, leaving weird and wonderful animals found nowhere else on Earth. In its jungles, a cassowary - one of the most dangerous birds in the world - stands six feet tall, while kangaroos and wombats brave snowstorms, and gum-tree forests are filled with predators. In its red desert heart, reptiles drink through their skin and huge flocks of wild budgerigars swirl in search of water. On secret islands Tasmanian devils roam, and offshore thousands of sharks gather for a rare event

programme.title: Fred Dibnahs Magnificent Monuments
programme.desc: Visits to historic places of work, including one of Britains oldest dockyards in Chatham Kent, and Armley Mill in Leeds. At a 1,000-year-old tithe barn in Sussex, the steeplejack tries his hand at thatching, before getting a surprise on the top floor of the architecturally esteemed Lloyds building in the City of London

programme.title: Spring Walks
programme.desc: Businesswoman and Dragons Den investor Sara Davies explores the Swinton Estate in North Yorkshire. Along the way Sara reveals the challenges she faced as a young businesswoman, the unexpected impact of participating in Strictly Come Dancing and the importance of family support

programme.title: Face the Music
programme.desc: Joseph Cooper invites viewers to match their musical wits against Joyce Grenfell, Paul Jennings and Robin Ray. With guest musician Gerald Moore. First broadcast in 1981

programme.title: Treasures of Ancient Egypt
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke looks at art from one of Ancient Egypts most opulent eras, beginning with the troubling psychological portraits of tyrant Senwosret III, who ruled during the Middle Kingdom, through to the golden mask of Tutankhamun, which was created hundreds of years later. He also explores architectural wonders, exquisite tombs and a lost city, where an artistic revolution took place and a new creative style was developed under Akhenaten and Nefertiti

programme.title: The Womens Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekends matches in the Super League, including Everton v Liverpool, Arsenal v Tottenham Hotspur and Manchester United v Manchester City

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Sunday with Laura Kuenssberg
programme.desc: Interviews with key politicians and public figures

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers looks at the political developments of the week and questions policy makers on the key issues

programme.title: Pilgrimage
programme.desc: The pilgrims head north-east to Moray on the edge of the Highlands and their first stop is Pluscarden Abbey, Britains only working medieval monastery. Keen to see how these current day Benedictine monks live and work, the pilgrims stay overnight, and sample some of the routines of monastic life, which includes helping with domestic duties

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Two bungalows in Derbyshire and Norfolk offer up lots of potential. Dion Dublin is impressed by the space on offer in a Liverpool mid-terrace

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents the show from Detling, Kent, with Stephanie Connell and John Cameron aiding the teams. Charlie also looks at a small collection dating from the 18th century

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley explores how the story of the Last Supper encapsulates so much of Jesus life, death and resurrection, and how it is significant about it for Christians today

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: An appeal for funds on behalf of a registered charity

programme.title: Gladiators
programme.desc: Bradley and Barney Walsh welcome back four finalist contenders to take on the Gladiators, and each other, for the title of champion in the grand final. Viper leaves the Gladiators gobsmacked, Bradley races Electro on the track, and much to Guy Mowbrays surprise, Legend tries his hand at commentary

programme.title: Crookhaven
programme.desc: Gabriel and Penelope face-off and go to extreme lengths to dig for dirt, while Ade and Ede learn that its risky to trust a crook

programme.title: Crookhaven
programme.desc: The school goes into lockdown when the Nameless attack. Gabriel and the gang are on a mission to discover the family secrets that Crookhaven is keeping hidden

programme.title: Attenboroughs Life in Colour
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores the many ways animals use colour, with specially built cameras revealing a world normally invisible to human eyes. The peacocks tail has evolved entirely to impress females and is one of the most extreme colour ornaments in the natural world, while mandrill baboons signal their strength and status with striking red and blue face masks, the brighter the colours, the higher ranking the male

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.sub-title: St Michaels Mount: After the Storm
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Anita Rani head to St Michaels Mount in Cornwall after Storm Goretti. Hurricane-force winds ripped through the island, leaving the tiny community of just 33 residents facing a huge clean-up. With Easter approaching, they join the race to repair the damage, restore the landscape and reopen this iconic landmark to visitors. Plus, Datshiane Navanayagam investigates the rise of youth homelessness in rural Britain

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents from Firstsite contemporary art gallery in Colchester, Essex, where treasures include a wartime wedding dress, an instruction book on the ancient art of paper folding, and a copper dish twice rescued from a skip. Fiona delves into the brutal Roman history of Colchester, and discovers the three items that have most influenced Miscellaneous and Collectables expert Mark Hill

programme.title: The Other Bennet Sister
programme.desc: Mary prepares for her official debut in London society, buoyed by her friendship with Ann and the attentions of Mr Ryder. But a run-in with her old rival Caroline Bingley threatens her growing confidence. Mary faces up to her old rival at a dinner party, while an urgent message from home throws everything into uncertainty

programme.title: The Other Bennet Sister
programme.desc: Mary rushes to Pemberley, fearing her mother is gravely ill, only to be thrown back into the demands of family life. She begins to lose hope of ever returning to London, until Mr Ryder unexpectedly turns up. His clear admiration for Mary helps the family to recognise how much she has changed. As excitement mounts over Mr Ryders visit, Mary finds herself on the brink of a momentous decision

programme.title: The Capture
programme.desc: Carey is under pressure to charge James Whitlock. Her failure to reach Napier leads to her facing the consequences of her actions, but an unlikely group of allies presents itself. Pierson must convince his colleagues that he is fit for the job, and an off-the-record military group seek advice from the mysterious Simon on how to get their plans back on track. A deadly trap is set for Rachel Care

programme.title: The Proposal (2009)
programme.desc: A Canadian publishing editor working in New York faces deportation to her own country. Desperate to stay in America, she offers her assistant a promotion if he will marry her, but an immigration official becomes suspicious and the pair are forced to go to desperate lengths to look like a happy couple. Romantic comedy, starring Sandra Bullock and Ryan Reynolds

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Angela Rippon hosts the satirical quiz, with comedian Jo Brand and writer and broadcaster Richard Osman joining team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop as they delve into the weeks news stories

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Four precious Amur tiger cubs are growing fast, but with only around 400 left in the wild, every step of their journey will be critical. The challenge for the keepers today is weighing the cubs, without causing stress or triggering aggression from their fiercely protective mum, Yana. Meanwhile, theres drama in Animal Adventure as Longleats century-old tortoises wake from their deep winter slumber

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.sub-title: Fields of Danger as Farmer Is Impaled and Eagles Threaten Highland Flocks
programme.desc: Documentary following the police, fire and ambulance services as they attend emergency situations in rural communities. The East Anglian Air Ambulance races to save a farmer after a freak accident, lambs face attack from birds of prey, and reporter Paul Colgan investigates illegal fly-tipping in Cheshire. Featuring accounts from first responders, community members and farmers at the centre of these emergencies

programme.title: Crimewatch Caught
programme.desc: The Flying Squad hunt for a brazen gang stealing jewellery and cash across the UK. And a daring heist of valuable rare books sets police off on an international manhunt

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.sub-title: Celebrity Special with Owain and Dianne Buswell
programme.desc: Strictly champion Dianne Buswell joins the team in this celebrity special, helping Owain Wyn Evans with the sale of a south London terrace, while Dion Dublin is in West Yorkshire, where he encounters some worrying-looking mould. Plus, a segment from the archives in which Martin Roberts visits a former chapel in Derbyshire

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles presents a round-up of highlights from the series featuring wooden antiques, catching up with red and blue teams across the country who bought some memorable pieces

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: Zoe Ball reveals how cutting smartphone use to just two hours a day can aid wellbeing, offering a busy beautician in Kettering some clever digital detox tricks

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: Pensioner David has decided to sell his rare and unique circus memorabilia and put the money raised towards a good cause - entertaining locals with his clown and variety act and helping support his poorly wife. After being greeted by a knights suit of armour, auctioneer Christina Trevanion makes museum-quality finds among Davids collection, including items that once belonged to famous circus owner PT Barnum. Meanwhile, JJ Chalmers finds an important and iconic piece of British innovation lying forgotten in a garden shed.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is in Cornwall, helping a couple to trade in city life for a coastal home. He also stops by a regenerative farm focused on nourishing land, body and mind

programme.title: The Repair Shop on the Road
programme.desc: Prestwicks art deco Broadway Cinema has not shown a film since its doors closed in 1976, so Mark Stuckey and Steve Rowley must battle rust, missing wires and obsolete carbon-arc lamps if they hope to bring its twin projectors back to life. Dom Chinea meets Shetlander Victoria, who needs her grandfathers accordion fixed if shes to realise her busking ambitions, and then discovers the centuries-old art of enamelling watch dials, a craft that demands steady hands

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp and Sarah Greene host the quiz in which five contestants race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try to win £5,000. The contenders battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple - to make it to the finish line before their rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the cash prize

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Rose Matafeo on the quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious answers to questions

programme.title: BBC News at Six; Weather
programme.desc: The latest headlines

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: JB Gill and Lauren Laverne present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Penny and Vinny have their first baby scan, Zoe tries to build bridges and Zack faces financial problems

programme.title: Ronan Keatings Wild Atlantic
programme.desc: Ronan continues his journey along Irelands Wild Atlantic coast with an emotional trip with his nephew to Clare Island, a special place for the entire Keating family. They get to grips with a traditional loom before Ronan heads for Sligo to meet up with a fellow boyband veteran, Shane Filan of Westlife, for some horse riding on the golden sands of Strandhill Beach

programme.title: Greatest Gardens with Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein
programme.desc: Guest judge Patrick Grant joins Diarmuid and Carol as they explore three more contenders for Northern Irelands Greatest Garden. They visit a coastal sanctuary in Millisle, a 15-acre woodland and wildlife garden in Ballymoney, and a country cottage with herbaceous borders and a cut flower garden in Greyabbey

programme.title: Babies
programme.desc: Drama, starring Paapa Essiedu and Siobhán Cullen as a couple dealing with the aftermath of losing a pregnancy. Lisa and Stephen try to get pregnant again, and a dinner exposes hidden tensions

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: A round-up of todays headlines

programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: Dangerous Dogs: Is The Ban Working? - Panorama
programme.desc: Two years ago, the Government banned XL Bully dogs, promising it would save lives. Reporter Sam Poling investigates whether that promise has been kept. Attacks are still happening, the police say the law is difficult to enforce, and some animal charities say they are overwhelmed with abandoned dogs. Sam hears from families whove lost loved ones and owners struggling to stay on the right side of the law

programme.title: Trying
programme.desc: With pressures mounting from all sides, Nikki and Jasons relationship comes under strain. Scott is not happy when Karen turns up for his book launch

programme.title: Trying
programme.desc: It is the day of the adoption hearing, and Nikki is feeling less than optimistic, so Jason proposes they get married while they are still together as a family

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Kate Humble and Ben Fogle explore Longleat Safari Park, meeting a host of exotic, fascinating animals and the people who look after them

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.sub-title: Inferno in the Fields and the Fight to Save Badgers
programme.desc: Police close in on an organised crime gang targeting farms and stealing high-tech GPS systems worth hundreds of thousands of pounds at farms across the north of England. In Leicestershire, a huge barn fire devastates on a family run dairy farm, threatening cattle and destroying vital winter feed. As the flames spread, farmer Blaise and daughter Millie race to save their livestock. In Scotland, reporter Paul Colgan meets farmer Debbie Duffus, as she is reunited with the air ambulance team members who saved her life after a brutal farm accident

programme.title: Crimewatch Caught
programme.desc: How the search for a vulnerable young woman unravelled a tragic case of neglect and murder. Plus, police investigating the murder of a popular teen found the key suspects very close to home. Narrated by Michelle Ackerley

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.sub-title: Celebrity Special with Martin and Scarlett Moffatt
programme.desc: Martin Roberts is joined by guest presenter Scarlett Moffatt to take a tour of a Sunderland property that has them looking to break out the champagne. Plus, clips from the archives, including a London studio flat and some dodgy patio doors

programme.title: Long Story Short
programme.desc: Desperate to impress his crush, awkward fresher Rohan turns to an online masculinity coach for advice, but begins to question if alpha status is all its cracked up to be

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Conor and Maireads much-loved fridge finally gives up on them. Remembering the item was originally bought in a town many miles away, Mairead decides that a family trip to that same place to buy a replacement will be just the thing to bond with her son. After some discussion Jock is eventually included, bringing along his keen navigation skills and unique sense of entertainment to pass the time. During the journey, all the travellers learn lessons about family connections. Comedy, starring Alex Murphy and Chris Walley

programme.title: Long Story Short
programme.desc: A shy 17-year-old outsider is marked for abduction as part of his new home towns terrifying tradition. He must evade capture and make it through the night, navigating love, friendship and events that are beyond explanation

programme.title: Long Story Short
programme.desc: A woman who has known she has a double since she was a 10 vows to confront her, and take back control of her life after a decade of intense and bitter rivalry

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Money is getting increasingly tight in the McSweeney household, prompting Conor and Jock to come up with inventive and unsatisfying ways of making food last longer. Determined to help Mairead financially, the lads think about ways they can come up with some extra cash, eventually arriving at an ill-advised plan. Meanwhile, Mairead and Sergeant Healy are in two minds about taking their friendship to the next level. Comedy, starring Alex Murphy and Chris Walley

programme.title: Long Story Short
programme.desc: An aspiring comedian must choose between sanitising his set and embracing his voice after a controversial performance results in further gigs being cancelled

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Conor and Jock make a plan to take a bus journey but not before Jock, posing as Fake Billy, swipes some alcopops from an off-licence and is unsuccessfully pursued by a security guard. Local bully Billy Murphy stumbles upon this chase, and follows his impersonator onto a bus which he proceeds to accidentally commandeer. Pursued by police, the wild ride ironically brings Conor, Jock and the girls closer together - along with the other innocent passengers. Comedy, starring Alex Murphy and Chris Walley

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Fortitude star Robert Sheehan appears as himself in this festive episode of the sitcom. The residents of the council estate where Conor and Jock live are threatened with eviction, but are not about to go down without a fight - and the resulting media circus puts everyone on edge. In the midst of all this mayhem, Conors mother Mairead is rushed to hospital, and may be more seriously ill than previously thought. When she says she wants to see a white Christmas, the lads vow to make her wish come true - with a little help from Sheehan, who is shooting a film nearby

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Documentary following boxer Tommy Furys return to the ring. Tommy is at his lowest point personally and professionally, living alone in a house that was meant to be his and partner Molly-Maes forever home. His role as a father to baby Bambi brings both joy and pressure as he tries to convince Molly-Mae he is a changed man. After finally getting the medical all-clear to fight again, Tommy and his trainer and father John prepare for his return to the ring against MMA fighter Darren Till. Team Fury travels to the press conference - with John promising to be on his best behaviour

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The Truemans prepare to welcome a visitor. After Suki dismisses Vinnys olive branch, Eve encourages her to put aside her differences. Bea puts on an overbearing act as Honey arrives home, and she later comes to blows with Linda who questions her motives

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Honey meets Bea in the cafe and asks her to move out of No.18, but Bea heads to McClunkys and orders everyone to leave before trashing the shop when her emotions get the better of her. Zoe is blindsided, while the Panesars and the Brannings attempt to bond

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Penny tells Vinny he doesnt need to buy her expensive gifts in return for her love, but she is shocked when Vinny reveals he has put a deposit down on a shop in Turpin Road for her. The Slaters put on a show of family unity, and Bea lies that she was attacked to garner sympathy

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Cindy finds herself in hot water, Bea orchestrates a plan, and Vicki supports Zack. Penny accepts Vinnys offer of a shop after words of encouragement from Gina and Harry. However, Suki accuses Penny of using Vinny for money

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out with Stacey Solomon
programme.sub-title: When You Find Out You Have 468 Wax Melts
programme.desc: The team arrive in the Midlands to help dads Craig and Craig, whose home is jam-packed with toys belonging to their beloved five-year-old daughter - not only the ones she plays with now, but everything she has ever owned, which salon owner Big Craig has kept hold of for fear of losing part of her childhood. But as the process begins, Stacey finds more clutter, from almost 200 pairs of shoes to 400 tampons - which Little Craig collected for the homeless but never got round to donating

programme.title: Boarders
programme.desc: Its final exams, and everyone is feeling the pressure. Leah is stressed that Felix will drag her down, but things are about to get a lot worse for them both. Toby makes a big play to try and cling on to his Cambridge offer and keep Abby. Femi is threatened by Cheddar, but Pixie helps him fight back. On the pitch, the hockey girls face their rivals, while Omars secret whips up the tension

programme.title: Boarders
programme.desc: Its nearly time for the boarders to say goodbye to St Gilberts. Femi is forced to choose between love and ambition, Omar finally tries to right his wrongs, and Toby makes a life-changing decision. With only one exam left to go, Leah cracks under the pressure. Can her friends bring her back from the brink? Final episode of the sitcom

programme.title: Long Story Short
programme.desc: Short films by new writers and directors. A woman from the West Country meets a man from the future, who might just hold the key to saving the planet. But the closer she gets to him, the more she suspects his story hides something darker

programme.title: Long Story Short
programme.desc: Lonely and disaffected Mabel finds a connection over a pineapple in the local supermarket. Comedy short by Rachelle Atalla, starring Harmony Rose Bremner and Dylan Blore

programme.title: Film Club
programme.desc: The day has finally come for Noa to leave, but Evie might not let him go that easily and it is not the only big decision she has to face

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Jock misses the first scan of his and Siobhans baby, so her parents come up with a plan to adopt the child. The suggestion doesnt go down well with anyone, so to test Jocks ability to look after a child, Principal Walsh challenges him to take care of an egg for a full month - without losing or breaking it. Comedy, starring Alex Murphy and Chris Walley

programme.title: Long Story Short
programme.desc: Consumed by his jealousy of his best friends sudden career success, Vasillys resentment festers, feeding a dark, supernatural force within him

programme.title: The Trials of Pa Salieu
programme.desc: Documentary following the British-Gambian musician as he attempts to rebuild his life and career after prison, returning to live performance while still on probation, navigating curfews, travel restrictions and the risk of recall. He makes his comeback at the Glastonbury Festival, but touring in Europe hangs in the balance, and he faces a choice between breaking commitments and risking a return to prison

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy receives news of the untimely death of boxing legend Ricky Hatton, which reminds him to live life to its fullest. He then sets out on the most gruelling test of endurance possible - climbing the three highest mountains in England, Scotland and Wales in just 24 hours. At Halloween, Tommy and John go pumpkin picking with Bambi, things are back on track with Molly, and the house renovation is almost completed

programme.title: Boarders
programme.desc: Its final exams, and everyone is feeling the pressure. Leah is stressed that Felix will drag her down, but things are about to get a lot worse for them both. Toby makes a big play to try and cling on to his Cambridge offer and keep Abby. Femi is threatened by Cheddar, but Pixie helps him fight back. On the pitch, the hockey girls face their rivals, while Omars secret whips up the tension

programme.title: Boarders
programme.desc: Its nearly time for the boarders to say goodbye to St Gilberts. Femi is forced to choose between love and ambition, Omar finally tries to right his wrongs, and Toby makes a life-changing decision. With only one exam left to go, Leah cracks under the pressure. Can her friends bring her back from the brink? Final episode of the sitcom

programme.title: Barra on the Foyle
programme.desc: Barra Best makes a journey across the river network, meeting people who work, live and play along its banks,and exploring places and landscapes rarely seen on TV screens

programme.title: Good Vibrations (2012)
programme.desc: A music lover in 1970s Belfast opens a record shop while his city is torn apart by the Troubles. His business introduces him to the citys underground punk scene, and he becomes one of the key figures in creating an alternative subculture to the violence around him. Fact-based drama, starring Richard Dormer, Jodie Whittaker, Liam Cunningham and Dylan Moran

programme.title: Show Tunes at the BBC
programme.desc: Elaine Paige guides viewers through a selection of songs from musicals from the BBC archives, including numbers from Cats, The Lion King, Funny Girl, My Fair Lady, Sunset Boulevard, West Side Story, A Little Night Music and The Sound of Music

programme.title: Sings Musicals
programme.desc: Memorable versions of songs taken from or inspired by musicals. The programme features Ella Fitzgerald singing Mack the Knife, from The Threepenny Opera, Captain Sensibles 1982 chart-topper Happy Talk, a cover of a song from South Pacific, and performances by Jeff Beck and Jay-Z

programme.title: The Capture
programme.desc: Carey searches for one suspect, while Frank Napier deals with another. As the evidence mounts and Carey is forced to avert another catastrophe, she begins to fear she might have made a terrible mistake. Piersons first day on the job gets ever more challenging, but as the net tightens for James Whitlock, it looks like SO15 might have made a breakthrough in the case

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood takes an alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture, beginning at the letter A with archive material from Gardeners World, Countryfile and The Gardeners Year

programme.title: Take a Hike
programme.desc: The first of five countryside walks in the Highlands and Islands, with teacher and army cadet trainer Daisy leading a hike up Stac Pollaidh, one of Scotlands best-known mountains

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: A look at life after dark on the North York Moors. At the Dark Skies Festival, Margherita Taylor finds out how light pollution is affecting nocturnal wildlife, while Sammi Kinghorn meets the people gathering vital dark-sky data and installing wildlife-friendly lighting to help protect the night. Plus, Adam Henson meets apprentices at a cattle mart meeting

programme.title: My Kind of Town
programme.desc: Ian Hamilton and his guide dog Major head to Campbeltown in Argyll and Bute, where he tries his hand at blind golf at the world-famous Machrihanish course and helps on the production line with the woman who made the chocolates for the Oscars gift bag

programme.title: My Kind of Town
programme.desc: Ian Hamilton and his guide dog Major visit Blairgowrie and Rattray in Perth and Kinross, asking those who live there how they feel about the place and what its future might hold

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents more highlights, with recipes from Niklas Ekstedt, Vivek Singh, Shivi Ramoutar and Big Zu, while singer Loyle Carner faces his food heaven or hell

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.sub-title: North East England and Yorkshire: Main and Dessert
programme.desc: The remaining three chefs from north east England and Yorkshire compete over mains and desserts inspired by the British film industry. The dishes are judged by veteran judge Paul Ainswort, which include a creation using duck, fresh fig and king oyster mushrooms to show what the kestrel in the classic film Kes might see whilst flying over the moors

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.sub-title: North East England and Yorkshire: Judging
programme.desc: Its judging day, and only one chef can go through to represent north east England and Yorkshire. Judges Tom Kerridge, Lorna McNee, and Phil Wang are joined by screenwriter and author Frank Cottrell-Boyce, who helps decide the winner from the two remaining chefs. Andi Oliver hosts

programme.title: Mary Berry - Love to Cook
programme.desc: Mary cooks four recipes that prove watching the pennies does not mean having to sacrifice on flavour. They include a crab courgetti spaghetti dish that uses tinned fish at a fraction of the cost of fresh. Plus, a spinach filo swirl demonstrates an inventive way of stretching six sausages to feed the family

programme.title: Figure Skating
programme.desc: Action from the annual event, held at O2 Arena in Prague, Czech Republic. A number of the stars of the rescent Winter Olympics in Italy were expected to be involved

programme.title: Evil Under the Sun (1982)
programme.desc: Hercule Poirot braves the heat and sand to investigate the murder of a glamorous stage star on an idyllic Adriatic holiday island. It becomes clear many people had reason to want the temperamental actress dead and the Belgian sleuth is soon hard at work figuring out which of her enemies eventually managed to kill her. Mystery, starring Peter Ustinov, Diana Rigg, James Mason, Maggie Smith and Jane Birkin.

programme.title: Big Cats 24/7
programme.desc: Reunited to protect themselves from intruding male lions, the Xudum pride mothers now struggle to feed so many mouths. Tsebe has four cubs, and her smallest is close to starving. When he becomes injured and then lost, his life is on a knife edge

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Callum and Zara believe last years heavy lamb losses were due in part to bird predation and are hoping new tactics will protect them. Having at last made it to Scotland, Lucy and Adam face yet more hurdles and tough decisions about the future of their dairy herd, while Emma and the kids run the farm while Rob is away and prepare to welcome visitors for Spring Fun Day

programme.title: Burma with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part one of a two-part documentary exploring the country in the aftermath of the recent humanitarian crisis in which thousands of Rohingya Muslims were driven from their homes by the military. Simon visits Burmas Buddhist heartlands, including the ancient capital of Bagan, and meets the citys controversial nationalist monks. While the region affected by the military crackdown is off limits, he is able to visit the worlds biggest refugee camp in Bangladesh, and meet people traumatised by the violence

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: A woman calls 999 to report that her 11-year-old daughter returned from staying at her dads house and claimed that a stranger came into her bedroom in the night and sexually assaulted her. A forensic team immediately heads to the crime scene, and the house is examined for potential forensic evidence the suspect may have left behind

programme.title: Tubaiste sa Phortach
programme.desc: Journalist Kevin Magee investigates the story of a Wellington bomber that took off from an RAF base in Limavady in February 1943 and crashed in Co Donegal, killing all six crew members. Kevin tracks down pilot Leonard Ashby Courts surviving son Ian in Leicestershire and accompanies the 84-year old as he fulfils a lifelong ambition by finally paying his respects at the site where his father died. In Irish

programme.title: Cumasc: Seisiúin sa Black Gate
programme.desc: French double bass virtuoso Renaud Garcia-Fons and Irish fiddler and composer Zoë Conway set forth on a journey of musical collaboration. In Irish

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers looks at the political developments of the week and questions policy makers on the key issues

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Bob Mortimer and Paul Whitehouse travel overseas for an Irish fishing trip on the River Finn, their first time angling in the waters of both the Republic of Ireland and Northern Ireland as they try to catch salmon. The Emerald Isle has some of the most prolific river runs for salmon in Western Europe, and its wet climate provides some of the best conditions for salmon fishing

programme.title: Chronicles of the Sperrins
programme.desc: Caroline from Conservation Detection Dogs NI along with Monty the spaniel are on a special mission to sniff out the location of an extremely rare native plant, which botanists are keen to study and protect. For night sky hunter Martin, summer sees him waiting hours to see if any magical noctilucent clouds will appear

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce hosts the topical debate Clacton-on-Sea, Essex, where the audience put questions to Justice Minister Jake Richards, the Conservative former Security Minister Tom Tugendhat, Layla Moran from the Liberal Democrats and businessman and TV personality Thomas Skinner

programme.title: Clash of the Superpowers: America vs China
programme.desc: The inside story of how America and China have become locked in a battle for global power and influence since Donald Trump first took office. The documentary begins with Trump inauguration in 2017, when he upended the USs relationship with Beijing. He promised to make China pay - but when he started a trade war, Xi Jinping was ready to fight back

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: Clive Myrie meets distillery manager Luke in Bristol to explain how swapping his coffee and energy drinks for tea could lower his stress levels, reduce anxiety, calm the brain and support heart health

programme.title: The Repair Shop on the Road
programme.desc: The team is in Clackmannanshire hoping to revive Alloa Co-op Bowling Clubs faded and warped sign in their centenary year. Meanwhile, Dom Chinea delivers a 19th-century regulator clock from the Hebrides with a ghostly connection, and Will Kirk heads north to Rothes in Moray to learn the craft behind forging traditional copper stills for the multibillion-dollar whisky industry

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion presents from Shepton Mallet, Somerset as Raj Bisram and Thomas Forrester help two teams find profitable antiques to sell at auction. Friends and neighbours Pat and Linda, lead by Raj Bisram, have sparkly items on their shopping list but are taken by a flamingo-headed walking cane. Sisters Jess and Jo, guided by Thomas Forrester, are looking for something that jumps out at them from the get-go

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Bike aficionado Tim Gunn tries to make a bent unicycle roadworthy once more for Heather from Winchester, who bought it as a gift for her late husband Perry. Mark Stuckey is entrusted with a canary yellow 1980s cassette player that belonged to owner Florences mum Mel. Other items are an Edwardian brass plate camera and a one-of-a-kind 3-D model of an adenovirus - the virus responsible for the common cold - made by an electron microscopist back in the 1960s

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: The UnXplained with William Shatner
programme.desc: An exploration of subjects that have mystified mankind for centuries, with the host shedding light on how the seemingly impossible can sometimes happen. Here, the veteran actor reflects on some remarkable tales of human survival against all the odds. Do such events reveal that humans have a hidden reserve of inexplicable energy locked away that only gets released when the situation calls for it?

programme.title: National Park Mysteries
programme.desc: The Hawaii Volcanoes National Park is the site of strange events said to be linked to the legendary Native Hawaiian Goddess of the Volcano, Pele

programme.title: National Park Mysteries

programme.desc: Experts scrutinise the engineering behind the Egyptians Sphinx and pyramids, questioning whether extra-terrestrial help was utilised to complete the structures

programme.desc: Reported accounts of genuine alien encounters, examining more previously off-limits information to investigate where in the universe UFOs could be coming from

programme.title: Alien Files: Unsealed
programme.desc: Investigating the reasons behind Nasas denials of UFO accounts and why the organisation has taken measures to prevent its astronauts from telling their stories

programme.title: Road Wars

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Seth is angered when Les dismisses the consultants suggestions about improving business practices. Seth disappears, and his manager fears he may be on the verge of quitting

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson joins antiques experts Karen Dalmeny, Stewart Hofgartner and Ian Towning in Worthing, West Sussex, to provide locals with the option of auctioning off their collectibles or accepting a cash sum. The star lot today is an oil painting that was found in an old house

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and antiques dealers Mike Melody, James Layte and Rob Bingham are in Nantwich, Cheshire, where a Della Robbia vase is the find of the day

programme.title: Blockbusters
programme.desc: Simon Mayo hosts the general knowledge quiz, in which contestants try to complete a path across or down a board of lettered hexagons

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Its a night of personality, passion and surprise pairings as Rebecca, a Boris Johnson fan, finds her political match in Bradley. Paris and Serena navigate chemistry and chaos over a spilled drink, while seaside-loving midwife Anna meets her sensitive surfer in Luke. Cormac, a vintage-obsessed 24-year-old, finds the heat with bold 58-year-old Lorraine

programme.title: Barbarian (2022)
programme.desc: In town for a job interview, a young woman arrives at her bed and breakfast late at night only to find that it has been mistakenly double-booked and a strange man is already staying there. Against her better judgement, she decides to stay the night anyway. Horror thriller, starring Georgina Campbell, Justin Long and Bill Skarsgard

programme.title: Ramsays Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay travels to Loudonville, Ohio, to rescue Landolls Mohican Castle, a custom-built, multimillion-dollar hotel set in an 1100-acre woodland estate. Co-owner Marta is struggling, having been left with the entire burden of running such a large establishment alone. Can Gordon persuade her son Jimmy to finally take the reins and turn this business around before the family legacy is destroyed?

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Another trilogy of spooky stories originally broadcast at Halloween. A Mayan civilisation - with some very familiar-looking members - predicts the end of the world, and a scientific experiment in Springfield ends up lumbering the town with its very own black hole - which threatens to swallow everything and everyone in sight. Finally, a spot of time travel relocates Bart to 1974 for a Back to the Future parody in which he inadvertently jeopardises his parents relationship

programme.title: Ramsays Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay travels to Loudonville, Ohio, to rescue Landolls Mohican Castle, a custom-built, multimillion-dollar hotel set in an 1100-acre woodland estate. Co-owner Marta is struggling, having been left with the entire burden of running such a large establishment alone. Can Gordon persuade her son Jimmy to finally take the reins and turn this business around before the family legacy is destroyed?

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart develops a crush on babysitter Laura Powers and plots to break up her relationship with bullying boyfriend Jimbo Jones - but things do not turn out as planned. Featuring the voice of Sara Gilbert (Roseanne, The Big Bang Theory)

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homers new snowplough business proves an unexpected success but drives a wedge between him and Barney, who decides to operate a rival service. Featuring the voices of Linda Ronstadt and Adam West

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien restore a metal workbench, a pine chest and the seat from an old pony and trap

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The family wonders when Maggie will say her first word, which prompts Marge to reminisce about Lisas early days and how a two-year-old Bart suffered a serious outbreak of sibling jealousy. Featuring the guest voice of Elizabeth Taylor

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer enters Lisa in a beauty pageant to boost her self-esteem, and has the satisfaction of seeing her crowned runner-up - but she finds her principles heavily compromised as a result. Featuring the voice of Bob Hope

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart misbehaves once too often, prompting Homer and Marge to impose a terrible punishment - banning him from ever going to see the new feature-length Itchy and Scratchy film, and standing firm despite his constant pleas

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge takes a job at the power plant to help with the cost of repairing the family home, which is sliding off its foundations. With the voice of Tom Jones

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Carrie and Doug fall out after suffering a bumpy aeroplane landing. Meanwhile, Spence has a date with a good-looking Brazilian exchange student - but things dont go as smoothly as he hoped. Kevin James and Leah Remini star

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug is called to testify as an expert witness for a case Carries working on - but she thinks he is being too light-hearted about it. Meanwhile, Danny and Spence fight over an attractive dog walker. Starring Kevin James and Leah Remini

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: The Heffernans start trying for a baby, but a visit from Dougs parents at the ideal time for conception leaves him struggling to perform. Starring Kevin James

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Amys parents draw attention to Roberts strange eating habits, making him feel self-conscious every time he sits down for a meal - so Debra devises a plan to cure him once and for all. Ray Romano, Patricia Heaton and Brad Garrett star

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: An invitation to spend Easter with the Barones spurs the MacDougalls to unite with the family over a 500-piece jigsaw puzzle - until Ray and Pat upset the apple cart by indulging in more than their fair share of bonding. Comedy, starring Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Part two of two. Niles clashes with girlfriend Mel over a misunderstanding, Daphne picks up the wrong signals, and friends are thin on the ground at Frasiers Christmas party. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The Cranes take a trip in the Winnebago on New Years Eve to celebrate the turn of the new millennium, but a case of mistaken identity leaves Frasier and Martin trying to rescue Niles from another familys van

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homers new snowplough business proves an unexpected success but drives a wedge between him and Barney, who decides to operate a rival service. Featuring the voices of Linda Ronstadt and Adam West

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The family wonders when Maggie will say her first word, which prompts Marge to reminisce about Lisas early days and how a two-year-old Bart suffered a serious outbreak of sibling jealousy. Featuring the guest voice of Elizabeth Taylor

programme.title: Sunday Brunch
programme.desc: Tim Lovejoy and Simon Rimmer are joined by guests Courtney Pine, Fatiha El-Ghorri, Richard Kind, Keith Brymer Jones, Marj Hogarth and Les Dennis. With music from the Pale White

programme.title: Formula 1
programme.desc: Steve Jones presents action from the third round of the season, held at Suzuka Circuit. Max Verstappen of Mercedes claimed his first win of the 2025 campaign here last year, with the McLaren duo of Lando Norris and Oscar Piastri finishing second and third respectively. Analysis from Billy Monger and Lawrence Baretto

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Danni Menzies is on Spains Costa Del Sol, which as one of Europes top golfing destinations is popularly known as the Costa De Golf. House hunter Peggy is a massive fan of the game and cant think of anywhere else shed rather buy a holiday home, so shes counting on Danni to find her the perfect property. For her £115,000 budget, Danni tees up five options for Peggy to see along with her mother Coral, but will any score a hole in one?

programme.title: George Clarkes Amazing Spaces
programme.sub-title: A Floating Home and a Magical Vista
programme.desc: A Nottingham couple cement their relationship with an ambitious floating home, while in London George discovers an explosion of global architecture in a tiny one-bedroom flat. In Lincolnshire, family relations are put to the test as two teenage siblings take on the task of building their own mobile homes away from home. Plus, together with master craftsman Will Hardie, George conjures up a magical 30-mile vista in a wall just 20cm thick in their secret garden studio flat

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Toy Story 2 (1999)
programme.desc: Cowboy doll Woody is stolen by an unscrupulous toy collector, who knows he is worth a fortune, prompting fearless Space Ranger Buzz Lightyear to lead a daring mission across town to rescue him. However, Woody becomes tempted to spend his life languishing in the safety of a museum rather than face the prospect of his owner growing up. Animated sequel, featuring the voices of Tom Hanks, Tim Allen, Joan Cusack and Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up to Cancer
programme.desc: Molly-Mae Hague, Richard Herring, JoJo Siwa and Babatunde Aleshe join hosts Noel Fielding and Alison Hammond and judges Cherish Finden and Paul Hollywood in the tent. The celebrity bakers take on decorated traybakes in the Signature and a tarte tatin Technical, then reveal their most coveted toys from childhood in showstopping biscuits

programme.title: The Hunt: Prey vs Predator
programme.desc: The Predators band together and ruthlessly round on one player, whose survival hangs in the balance. Meanwhile, one of the contests closest alliances is put in peril. There is an unexpected change of fortune, as one player goes from underdog to top dog. After one of the toughest hunts yet, the players return to base, where politics and power plays kick in, before another of the group is culled from the contest

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The Goggleboxers settle in for The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up to Cancer, Saturday Night Live UK, Britains Got Talent, The Hunt: Prey vs Predator, Celebrity Sabotage, Something Very Bad Is Going to Happen, The Dog House and BBC News on Easter egg inflation

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up to Cancer
programme.desc: Molly-Mae Hague, Richard Herring, JoJo Siwa and Babatunde Aleshe join hosts Noel Fielding and Alison Hammond and judges Cherish Finden and Paul Hollywood in the tent. The celebrity bakers take on decorated traybakes in the Signature and a tarte tatin Technical, then reveal their most coveted toys from childhood in showstopping biscuits

programme.title: xXx (2002)
programme.desc: Extreme sports star Xander Cage finds himself in trouble when his daredevil exploits land him behind bars. However, he is offered an intriguing escape route, courtesy of National Security Agent Augustus Gibbons - who thinks the adrenaline junkie is the perfect man to bring down a gang of international terrorists in Prague. Action adventure, starring Vin Diesel, Samuel L Jackson, Asia Argento, Marton Csokas and Michael Roof

programme.title: Ramsays Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay uses his wealth of experience in the restaurant business to help struggling hotels, motels and BandBs across America. After identifying the most pressing issues at each location, he puts the owners and staff to work as he attempts to save their establishments from disaster. His first stop is Coeur dAlene, Idaho, where the Roosevelt Inn is more than $1million in debt - and to make matters worse, the owners wife has had enough

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge experiences mixed emotions when she is forced to replace the family car after an encounter with a sinkhole, while Bart becomes interested in Lisas extracurricular activities after finding out about her covert meetings in town. With the guest voice of stock car racer Jeff Gordon

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart develops a crush on babysitter Laura Powers and plots to break up her relationship with bullying boyfriend Jimbo Jones - but things do not turn out as planned. Featuring the voice of Sara Gilbert (Roseanne, The Big Bang Theory)

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homers new snowplough business proves an unexpected success but drives a wedge between him and Barney, who decides to operate a rival service. Featuring the voices of Linda Ronstadt and Adam West

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The family wonders when Maggie will say her first word, which prompts Marge to reminisce about Lisas early days and how a two-year-old Bart suffered a serious outbreak of sibling jealousy. Featuring the guest voice of Elizabeth Taylor

programme.title: Sunday Brunch
programme.desc: Highlights of the food and chat show, with Tim Lovejoy and Simon Rimmer

programme.title: George Clarkes Old House, New Home
programme.desc: George Clarke helps more people make their period homes fit for modern life. On a cobbled street in Cheshire sits a Georgian town house that could be straight out of a Dickens novel. It may look very 18th-century on the outside, but inside George discovers that its a 1980s horror show, having been stripped of its original features. It does have one unique feature, though - a first-floor conservatory on stilts. Can he find a way of getting its grandeur back? George also has his work cut out tackling a 380-year-old Jacobean cottage

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the words-and-numbers game, with Jon Kay joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Norm angles for promotion by arranging for Diane to date his playboy boss - but neglects to tell her about it beforehand. Bar-room comedy, starring Ted Danson and George Wendt

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Sam decides hes had enough of the continual bickering between Diane and Carla and tells them they must resolve the bitter feelings between the pair. Ted Danson stars

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Coach develops a foolproof technique for wooing women and astonishes everyone by vying with Sam for the attentions of a new neighbour. Ted Danson and Nicholas Colasanto star

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray is upset to hear Debra has been discussing intimate details of their marriage with her gossipy friends - and responds by revealing a few secrets of his own. Guest starring Maggie Wheeler (Friends)

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Robert is elated to be signed up by a modelling agency, convinced fame and wealth are just around the corner - but his dreams of success prove short-lived. Comedy, starring Ray Romano and Brad Garrett

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: A stranger reveals to the Barones that Frank used to be his mentor - much to the bemusement of Ray and Robert, who find it hard to believe their father could ever have inspired anyone. Comedy, starring Ray Romano, Peter Boyle and Brad Garrett

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The shrink pulls out all the stops to compose a new theme tune for his radio show - but fails to appreciate what is required. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The shrink is delighted to land a date with a former prom queen, but soon learns the hard way that beauty is only skin deep. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: An unlikely sequence of events culminates in Martin pretending to be gay on Valentines Day. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and John Mahoney

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: A temporary call-screener steals the show with her own brand of no-nonsense advice, leaving Frasier feeling insecure. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay applies his gastronomic and business acumen to Simmer Down Cafe in Georgetown, Texas, whose owner Chris Marino wishes to attract a younger, richer clientele. Chris has asked his brother Albert to come out of retirement to help with the business, but struggles with the notion that things can function properly without his presence

programme.title: Channel 4 News Summary
programme.desc: Midday news summary

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon OBrien and Henry Cole head to north Wales, where things kick off with a bang when Henry finds an antique cannon in antique dealers Sue and Joshs storage shed. Simon finds a croquet set in need of restoration and shows his restorer Gemma how to play the game

programme.title: Matt Baker: Our Farm in the Dales
programme.desc: Return of the documentary following Matt Baker and his family. The clans farm is more manageable than ever before, but as the worst storm to hit the area in living memory strikes, many of the changes theyve made have been destroyed

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Mike Bubbins in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against words and numbers

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jill and Ronnie have dreamt of moving to France for many years. Now their children are all grown up, the time seems right for them to take this big step. Jasmine Harman lines up five options for their £250,000 budget. Will Jill and Ronnie love any of them enough to make one their new home?

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: In Italy, Lisa and Paul open two holiday let apartments. In Portugal, friends Richard and Simon call on the community to raise the funds to lease and renovate a much-loved abandoned bar. In France, couple Mark and Gary begin to renovate an old cognac distillery into a high-end luxury gîte

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first destination is 527-year-old pub, the Crown Inn in Kent, where guests experience spooky tales of the old boozers spectral resident and a boisterous bout of drag bingo

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Content creator Lizzy is first to host in Sheffield, serving up a Mexican-themed night involving a build-your-own burrito bonanza for mains and an unexpected surprise for dessert

programme.title: Jasmine Harmans Renovation in the Sun
programme.desc: The property expert and her family transform a run-down Spanish villa. In this first episode, Jasmine and husband Jon show their children the villa that they hope to call home. But with various delays and a mortgage deadline, its a race against the clock to see if the purchase of the villa will even go through

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Batch from Scratch: Cooking for Less
programme.desc: Joe Swash and Suzanne Mulholland head to Nottingham to help a busy NHS family with two energetic children whose mealtimes are completely out of control. Emma is juggling shift work, clubs, cooking and a painful spinal condition, while Matts long shifts mean he rarely cooks. Their hectic schedule has left them short on time, short on energy and spending almost £400 a week on food, with 22 shopping trips in a week

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Doctors treat 15-year-old Meldred, who has been stabbed in the thigh by a stranger while walking to school, and are concerned that the knife may have caused significant damage or internal bleeding. The medical staff also try to diagnose what is wrong with sports commentator Andrew, who has an inflamed ankle with blood spots and a rash, and treat 11-year-old Penelope, who has hurt her finger falling in the playground

programme.title: The Hunt: Prey vs Predator
programme.desc: Predator turns on Predator, as one player puts friendship above the brutal demands of the game. But will their loyalty be rewarded, or seal their fate in the cull? As another player goes all-out to undermine their biggest threat, feuds intensify, leading to a shocking turn of events during the cull and sparking a fiery, emotional clash that sets new rivalries alight

programme.title: Sphinx
programme.desc: Dutch mystery thriller about a teenager who reappears after having been missing for years, the only clue to what has happened a tattoo on her back. She remains unaware that her kidnapper has allowed her to escape, and blinded her trauma, she fulfils her role in his plan. Starring Rifka Lodeizen. In Dutch and French
